cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.\n\n## Quality bar\n\nYou are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher power class. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.\n\nSpecifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod actor_scope;\\n45\\tpub mod auth;\\n46\\tmod collection_listing;\\n47\\tpub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\\n48\\tpub mod control_plane;\\n49\\tpub mod control_plane_routes;\\n50\\tpub mod schema_registry;\\n58\\tpub mod serve;\\n59\\tpub mod service;\\n60\\tpub mod tenant_router;\\n61\\t\\n62\\tpub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\\n63\\tpub use Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\\n1\\t//!\\n2\\t//! # Layers\\n3\\t//!\\n4\\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\\n5\\t//!\\n6\\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`) control access to\\n7\\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\\n8\\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\\n9\\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\\n10\\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\\n11\\t//!\\n12\\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\\n15\\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\\n16\\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\\n17\\t//!\\n18\\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\\n21\\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\\n22\\t//!\\n23\\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\\n24\\t//!\\n25\\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\\\"*\\\"` for all databases).\\n26\\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\\n27\\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\\n28\\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\\n29\\t\\n30\\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\n31\\t\\n32\\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\\n33\\t\\n34\\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\\n35\\t///\\n36\\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`.\\n37\\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\\n38\\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\\n39\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n40\\tpub enum Role {\\n41\\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\\n42\\t    None = 0,\\n43\\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\\n44\\t    Read = 1,\\n45\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\\n46\\t    Write = 2,\\n47\\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\\n48\\t    Admin = 3,\\n49\\t}\\n50\\t\\n51\\timpl Role {\\n52\\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\\n53\\t    ///\\n54\\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\\n55\\t    ///\\n56\\t    /// | Input | Role |\\n57\\t    /// |-------|------|\\n58\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-admin\\\"` / `\\\"tag:admin\\\"` / `\\\"admin\\\"` | `Admin` |\\n59\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-write\\\"` / `\\\"tag:axon-agent\\\"` / `\\\"tag:write\\\"` / `\\\"write\\\"` | `Write` |\\n60\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-read\\\"` / `\\\"tag:read\\\"` / `\\\"read\\\"` | `Read` |\\n61\\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\\n62\\t    ///\\n63\\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` — the conventional tag for\\n64\\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\\n65\\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: \u0026str) -\u003e Self {\\n66\\t        match tag {\\n67\\t            \\\"tag:axon-admin\\\" | \\\"tag:admin\\\" | \\\"admin\\\" =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n68\\t            \\\"tag:axon-write\\\" | \\\"tag:axon-agent\\\" | \\\"tag:write\\\" | \\\"write\\\" =\u003e Role::Write,\\n69\\t            \\\"tag:axon-read\\\" | \\\"tag:read\\\" | \\\"read\\\" =\u003e Role::Read,\\n70\\t            _ =\u003e Role::None,\\n71\\t        }\\n72\\t    }\\n73\\t\\n74\\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\\n75\\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: \u0026[String]) -\u003e Self {\\n76\\t        tags.iter()\\n77\\t            .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\\n78\\t            .max()\\n79\\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\\n80\\t    }\\n81\\t}\\n82\\t\\n83\\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\\n84\\t    fn fmt(\u0026self, f: \u0026mut std::fmt::Formatter\u003c'_\u003e) -\u003e std::fmt::Result {\\n85\\t        match self {\\n86\\t            Role::None =\u003e write!(f, \\\"none\\\"),\\n87\\t            Role::Read =\u003e write!(f, \\\"read\\\"),\\n88\\t            Role::Write =\u003e write!(f, \\\"write\\\"),\\n89\\t            Role::Admin =\u003e write!(f, \\\"admin\\\"),\\n90\\t        }\\n91\\t    }\\n92\\t}\\n93\\t\\n94\\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\\n95\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\\n96\\tpub enum Operation {\\n97\\t    /// Read an entity, query, traverse, audit.\\n98\\t    Read,\\n99\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities/links.\\n100\\t    Write,\\n101\\t    /// Drop collections, modify schemas, manage namespaces.\\n102\\t    Admin,\\n103\\t}\\n104\\t\\n105\\timpl Operation {\\n106\\t    /// The minimum role required for this operation.\\n107\\t    pub fn required_role(self) -\u003e Role {\\n108\\t        match self {\\n109\\t            Operation::Read =\u003e Role::Read,\\n110\\t            Operation::Write =\u003e Role::Write,\\n111\\t            Operation::Admin =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n112\\t        }\\n113\\t    }\\n114\\t}\\n115\\t\\n116\\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\\n117\\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\\n118\\t///\\n119\\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\\n120\\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\\n121\\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\\n122\\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\\n123\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n124\\tpub struct EntityFilter {\\n125\\t    /// The top-level data field to compare.\\n126\\t    pub field: String,\\n127\\t    /// The required value for that field.\\n128\\t    pub value: serde_json::Value,\\n129\\t}\\n130\\t\\n131\\timpl EntityFilter {\\n132\\t    /// Convenience constructor.\\n133\\t    pub fn new(field: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, value: serde_json::Value) -\u003e Self {\\n134\\t        Self {\\n135\\t            field: field.into(),\\n136\\t            value,\\n137\\t        }\\n138\\t    }\\n139\\t\\n140\\t    /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\\n141\\t    ///\\n142\\t    /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\\n143\\t    /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\\n144\\t    pub fn matches(\u0026self, data: \u0026serde_json::Value) -\u003e bool {\\n145\\t        data.get(\u0026self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == \u0026self.value)\\n146\\t    }\\n147\\t}\\n148\\t\\n149\\t/// Identity context for a request.\\n150\\t///\\n151\\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\\n152\\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\\n153\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n154\\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\\n155\\t    /// Actor name for audit log entries.\\n156\\t    pub actor: String,\\n157\\t    /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\\n158\\t    pub role: Role,\\n159\\t    /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\\n160\\t    /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). When `Some`, this caller may only mutate entities\\n161\\t    /// whose data matches the filter. `None` means no scope restriction.\\n162\\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \\\"Option::is_none\\\")]\\n163\\t    pub entity_filter: Option\u003cEntityFilter\u003e,\\n164\\t}\\n165\\t\\n166\\timpl CallerIdentity {\\n167\\t    /// Create an anonymous identity with admin privileges (`--no-auth` mode).\\n168\\t    pub fn anonymous() -\u003e Self {\\n169\\t        Self {\\n170\\t            actor: \\\"anonymous\\\".into(),\\n171\\t            role: Role::Admin,\\n172\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n173\\t        }\\n174\\t    }\\n175\\t\\n176\\t    /// Create an identity from a name and role.\\n177\\t    pub fn new(actor: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, role: Role) -\u003e Self {\\n178\\t        Self {\\n179\\t            actor: actor.into(),\\n180\\t            role,\\n181\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n182\\t        }\\n183\\t    }\\n184\\t\\n185\\t    /// Attach an entity scope filter (builder style, FEAT-022).\\n186\\t    pub fn with_entity_filter(mut self, filter: EntityFilter) -\u003e Self {\\n187\\t        self.entity_filter = Some(filter);\\n188\\t        self\\n189\\t    }\\n190\\t\\n191\\t    /// Check whether this caller is authorized for the given operation.\\n192\\t    ///\\n193\\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authorized, or `Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation)`\\n194\\t    /// with a descriptive message if not.\\n195\\t    pub fn check(\u0026self, op: Operation) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n196\\t        let required = op.required_role();\\n197\\t        if self.role \u003e= required {\\n198\\t            Ok(())\\n199\\t        } else {\\n200\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n201\\t                \\\"permission denied: role '{}' cannot perform {:?} operations (requires '{}')\\\",\\n202\\t                self.role, op, required\\n203\\t            )))\\n204\\t        }\\n205\\t    }\\n206\\t\\n207\\t    /// Apply field-level masking to entity data based on mask policies.\\n208\\t    ///\\n209\\t    /// Admin users see all fields. For other roles, fields listed in a\\n210\\t    /// mask policy that requires a higher role than the caller's are\\n211\\t    /// removed from the data.\\n212\\t    pub fn apply_masks(\u0026self, data: \u0026mut serde_json::Value, policies: \u0026[MaskPolicy]) {\\n213\\t        if self.role \u003e= Role::Admin {\\n214\\t            return; // Admin sees all.\\n215\\t        }\\n216\\t        if let Some(obj) = data.as_object_mut() {\\n217\\t            for policy in policies {\\n218\\t                if self.role \u003c policy.min_role {\\n219\\t                    obj.remove(\u0026policy.field);\\n220\\t                }\\n221\\t            }\\n222\\t        }\\n223\\t    }\\n224\\t}\\n225\\t\\n226\\t/// A field-level mask policy (US-046, FEAT-012).\\n227\\t///\\n228\\t/// Specifies that a field should be hidden from callers whose role is\\n229\\t/// below `min_role`. Admin always sees all fields.\\n230\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n231\\tpub struct MaskPolicy {\\n232\\t    /// The field name to mask.\\n233\\t    pub field: String,\\n234\\t    /// Minimum role required to see this field.\\n235\\t    pub min_role: Role,\\n236\\t}\\n237\\t\\n238\\t// ── Attribute-based write control (US-047, FEAT-012) ────────────────────────\\n239\\t\\n240\\t/// Per-collection write policy for attribute-based access control (ABAC).\\n241\\t///\\n242\\t/// Stored in the `__axon_policies__` pseudo-collection. Defines:\\n243\\t/// - Minimum role required to write to this collection\\n244\\t/// - Fields that are immutable after creation\\n245\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n246\\tpub struct WritePolicy {\\n247\\t    /// Collection this policy applies to.\\n248\\t    pub collection: String,\\n249\\t    /// Minimum role required for write operations.\\n250\\t    pub min_write_role: Role,\\n251\\t    /// Fields that cannot be modified after the initial create.\\n252\\t    #[serde(default)]\\n253\\t    pub immutable_fields: Vec\u003cString\u003e,\\n254\\t}\\n255\\t\\n256\\timpl WritePolicy {\\n257\\t    /// Check if a caller has sufficient role to write to this collection.\\n258\\t    pub fn check_write(\u0026self, caller: \u0026CallerIdentity) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n259\\t        if caller.role \u003e= self.min_write_role {\\n260\\t            Ok(())\\n261\\t        } else {\\n262\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n263\\t                \\\"write policy for '{}': role '{}' insufficient (requires '{}')\\\",\\n264\\t                self.collection, caller.role, self.min_write_role\\n265\\t            )))\\n266\\t        }\\n267\\t    }\\n268\\t\\n269\\t    /// Check if any immutable fields have been modified.\\n270\\t    ///\\n271\\t    /// Returns the list of field names that were illegally modified.\\n272\\t    pub fn check_immutable_fields(\\n273\\t        \u0026self,\\n274\\t        old_data: \u0026serde_json::Value,\\n275\\t        new_data: \u0026serde_json::Value,\\n276\\t    ) -\u003e Vec\u003cString\u003e {\\n277\\t        let mut violations = Vec::new();\\n278\\t        for field in \u0026self.immutable_fields {\\n279\\t            let old_val = old_data.get(field);\\n280\\t            let new_val = new_data.get(field);\\n281\\t            // If the field existed before and is now different or missing, it's a violation.\\n282\\t            if old_val.is_some() \u0026\u0026 old_val != new_val {\\n283\\t                violations.push(field.clone());\\n284\\t            }\\n285\\t        }\\n286\\t        violations\\n287\\t    }\\n288\\t}\\n289\\t\\n290\\t// ── Database-scoped access control (US-038, FEAT-014) ────────────────────────\\n291\\t\\n292\\t/// A grant that scopes a role to a specific database.\\n293\\t///\\n294\\t/// Without a grant, users cannot access a database (unless they have a\\n295\\t/// wildcard `*` grant or admin role).\\n296\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n297\\tpub struct DatabaseGrant {\\n298\\t    /// The actor (user/node) this grant applies to. `\\\"*\\\"` means all users.\\n299\\t    pub actor: String,\\n300\\t    /// The database name. `\\\"*\\\"` means all databases.\\n301\\t    pub database: String,\\n302\\t    /// The role granted for this database.\\n303\\t    pub role: Role,\\n304\\t}\\n305\\t\\n306\\t/// Registry of database-scoped grants.\\n307\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n308\\tpub struct GrantRegistry {\\n309\\t    grants: Vec\u003cDatabaseGrant\u003e,\\n310\\t}\\n311\\t\\n312\\timpl GrantRegistry {\\n313\\t    /// Create a new empty grant registry.\\n314\\t    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n315\\t        Self::default()\\n316\\t    }\\n317\\t\\n318\\t    /// Add a grant.\\n319\\t    pub fn add(\u0026mut self, grant: DatabaseGrant) {\\n320\\t        self.grants.push(grant);\\n321\\t    }\\n322\\t\\n323\\t    /// Remove all grants for an actor on a database.\\n324\\t    pub fn revoke(\u0026mut self, actor: \u0026str, database: \u0026str) {\\n325\\t        self.grants\\n326\\t            .retain(|g| g.actor != actor || g.database != database);\\n327\\t    }\\n328\\t\\n329\\t    /// Resolve the effective role for an actor on a database.\\n330\\t    ///\\n331\\t    /// Checks grants in order: exact match, then wildcard actor, then\\n332\\t    /// wildcard database. Returns the highest-privilege matching role.\\n333\\t    pub fn effective_role(\u0026self, actor: \u0026str, database: \u0026str) -\u003e Role {\\n334\\t        self.grants\\n335\\t            .iter()\\n336\\t            .filter(|g| {\\n337\\t                (g.actor == actor || g.actor == \\\"*\\\")\\n338\\t                    \u0026\u0026 (g.database == database || g.database == \\\"*\\\")\\n339\\t            })\\n340\\t            .map(|g| g.role)\\n341\\t            .max()\\n342\\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\\n343\\t    }\\n344\\t\\n345\\t    /// Check if an actor can perform an operation on a database.\\n346\\t    pub fn check(\\n347\\t        \u0026self,\\n348\\t        actor: \u0026str,\\n349\\t        database: \u0026str,\\n350\\t        operation: Operation,\\n351\\t    ) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n352\\t        let role = self.effective_role(actor, database);\\n353\\t        if role \u003e= operation.required_role() {\\n354\\t            Ok(())\\n355\\t        } else {\\n356\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n357\\t                \\\"no grant for actor '{actor}' on database '{database}' with sufficient role (has '{role}', needs '{}')\\\",\\n358\\t                operation.required_role()\\n359\\t            )))\\n360\\t        }\\n361\\t    }\\n362\\t\\n363\\t    /// List all grants.\\n364\\t    pub fn list(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026[DatabaseGrant] {\\n365\\t        \u0026self.grants\\n366\\t    }\\n367\\t}\\n368\\t\\n369\\t#[cfg(test)]\\n370\\tmod tests {\\n371\\t    use super::*;\\n372\\t\\n373\\t    #[test]\\n374\\t    fn role_ordering() {\\n375\\t        assert!(Role::Admin \u003e Role::Write);\\n376\\t        assert!(Role::Write \u003e Role::Read);\\n377\\t        assert!(Role::Read \u003e Role::None);\\n378\\t    }\\n379\\t\\n380\\t    #[test]\\n381\\t    fn role_from_tag() {\\n382\\t        // Primary tags\\n383\\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\\\"tag:axon-admin\\\"), Role::Admin);\\n384\\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\\\"tag:axon-write\\\"), Role::Write);\\n385\\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\\\"tag:axon-read\\\"), Role::Read);\\n386\\t        // Short-form aliases\\n387\\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\\\"tag:admin\\\"), Role::Admin);\\n388\\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\\\"tag:write\\\"), Role::Write);\\n389\\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\\\"tag:read\\\"), Role::Read);\\n390\\t        // Bare names (no tag: prefix)\\n391\\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\\\"admin\\\"), Role::Admin);\\n392\\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\\\"write\\\"), Role::Write);\\n393\\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\\\"read\\\"), Role::Read);\\n394\\t        // Agent alias\\n395\\t        assert_eq!(Role::from_tag(\\\"tag:axon-agent\\\"), Role::Write);\\n396\\t        // Unknown\\n397\\t \u0026default_namespace.schema,\\n                ],\\n            ))?;\\n        }\\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\\n            \\\"DELETE FROM collections\\n                 WHERE name = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\\\",\\n            \u0026[\\n                \u0026key.collection.as_str(),\\n                \u0026key.namespace.database,\\n                \u0026key.namespace.schema,\\n            ],\\n        ))?;\\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\\n                \\\"DELETE FROM collections\\n                     WHERE name = $1\\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\\\",\\n                \u0026[\\n                    \u0026raw_collection,\\n                    \u0026key.namespace.database,\\n                    \u0026key.namespace.schema,\\n                    \u0026default_namespace.database,\\n                    \u0026default_namespace.schema,\\n                ],\\n            ))?;\\n        }\\n        Ok(())\\n    }\\n\\n    fn list_collections(\u0026self) -\u003e Result\u003cVec\u003cCollectionId\u003e, AxonError\u003e {\\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\\n            \\\"SELECT name FROM collections\\n                 ORDER BY database_name ASC, schema_name ASC, name ASC\\\",\\n            \u0026[],\\n        ))?;\\n\\n        Ok(rows\\n            .iter()\\n            .map(|row| {\\n                let name: String = row.get(\\\"name\\\");\\n                CollectionId::new(name)\\n            })\\n            .collect())\\n    }\\n\\n    fn collection_registered_in_namespace(\\n self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\\n///\\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if !exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"DROP DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\\n// Explicit verification path:\\n// postgres_test_guard() -\u003e MutexGuard\u003c'static, ()\u003e {\\n        static GUARD: OnceLock\u003cMutex\u003c()\u003e\u003e = OnceLock::new();\\n        GUARD\\n            .get_or_init(|| storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    inner: Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\\n28\\t/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\\n29\\tpub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -\u003e Response {\\n30\\t    let raw = uri.path();\\n31\\t    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\\n32\\t    let asset_path = raw\\n33\\t        .strip_prefix(\\\"/ui/\\\")\\n34\\t        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\\\"/ui\\\"))\\n35\\t        .unwrap_or(raw);\\n36\\t    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\\n37\\t        \\\"index.html\\\"\\n38\\t    } else {\\n39\\t        asset_path\\n40\\t    };\\n41\\t\\n42\\t    serve_asset(asset_path)\\n43\\t}\\n44\\t\\n45\\tfn serve_asset(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Response {\\n46\\t    match UiAssets::get(path) {\\n47\\t        Some(content) =\u003e {\\n48\\t            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\\n49\\t            Response::builder()\\n50\\t                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\\n51\\t                .body(Body::from(content.data))\\n52\\t                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\\n53\\t        }\\n54\\t        None =\u003e {\\n55\\t            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\\n56\\t            match UiAssets::get(\\\"index.html\\\") {\\n57\\t                Some(index) =\u003e Response::builder()\\n58\\t                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \\\"text/html; charset=utf-8\\\")\\n59\\t                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\\n60\\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\\n61\\t                None =\u003e StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\\n62\\t            }\\n63\\t        }\\n64\\t    }\\n65\\t}\\n66\\t\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"639a3f3a-e27d-4d23-b9e3-a0b9bc507e2b\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:45:23.541Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/src/embedded_ui.rs\",\"content\":\"//! directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. package agent

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type interruptedAttemptGitRun struct {
	projectRoot        string
	store              *bead.Store
	candidate          *bead.Bead
	result             *ExecuteBeadLoopResult
	err                error
	attemptID          string
	runStateRootRel    string
	runStateAttemptRel string
}

func TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptCommitsTrackerCleanup(t *testing.T) {
	run := runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t, false)

	require.ErrorIs(t, run.err, context.Canceled)
	require.NotNil(t, run.result)
	require.Len(t, run.result.Results, 1)

	got, err := run.store.Get(context.Background(), run.candidate.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
	assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)

	status := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--",
		".ddx/beads.jsonl",
		".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl",
		".ddx/attachments",
	)
	assert.Empty(t, status, "interrupted cleanup must not leave tracker/audit paths dirty")

	subject := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "log", "-1", "--pretty=%s")
	assert.Equal(t, "chore: update tracker (execute-bead "+run.attemptID+")", subject)

	show := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "show", "--name-only", "--format=", "HEAD")
	assert.Contains(t, show, ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
}

func TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptDoesNotCommitRunState(t *testing.T) {
	run := runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t, true)

	require.ErrorIs(t, run.err, context.Canceled)
	require.NotNil(t, run.result)

	show := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "show", "--name-only", "--format=", "HEAD")
	assert.NotContains(t, show, run.runStateRootRel)
	assert.NotContains(t, show, run.runStateAttemptRel)

	status := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--", run.runStateRootRel, run.runStateAttemptRel)
	assert.Contains(t, status, run.runStateRootRel)
	assert.Contains(t, status, run.runStateAttemptRel)

	trackerStatus := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--",
		".ddx/beads.jsonl",
		".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl",
		".ddx/attachments",
	)
	assert.Empty(t, trackerStatus, "tracker cleanup commit must still land while run-state stays dirty")
}

func TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptDoesNotRecordTerminalOutcome(t *testing.T) {
	run := runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t, false)

	require.ErrorIs(t, run.err, context.Canceled)
	require.NotNil(t, run.result)
	require.Len(t, run.result.Results, 1)

	report := run.result.Results[0]
	assert.True(t, report.Disrupted)
	assert.Equal(t, "context_canceled", report.DisruptionReason)
	assert.Empty(t, report.RetryAfter)

	got, err := run.store.Get(context.Background(), run.candidate.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
	assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)
	assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
	_, hasRetry := got.Extra["work-retry-after"]
	assert.False(t, hasRetry, "cancelled attempts must remain immediately retryable")

	events, err := run.store.Events(run.candidate.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	for _, ev := range events {
		if ev.Kind != "execute-bead" {
			continue
		}
		assert.NotContains(t,
			[]string{ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied, ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges},
			ev.Summary,
			"interrupted cleanup must not record a terminal outcome event",
		)
	}
}

func runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t *testing.T, trackDirtyRunState bool) interruptedAttemptGitRun {
	t.Helper()

	projectRoot := newDurableAuditProject(t)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
	store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))

	candidate := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-interrupted-attempt", Title: "Interrupted attempt", Priority: 0}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), candidate))

	attemptID := "20260515T160340-interrupted"
	runStateRootRel := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ddxroot.DirName, RunStateFileName))
	runStateAttemptRel := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ddxroot.DirName, RunStateDirName, attemptID+".json"))

	if trackDirtyRunState {
		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, RunStateDirName), 0o755))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(runStateRootRel)), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"seed"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(runStateAttemptRel)), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"seed"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
	}

	runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "add", ".")
	if trackDirtyRunState {
		runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "add", "-f", runStateRootRel, runStateAttemptRel)
	}
	runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed interrupted attempt cleanup")
	head := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")

	if trackDirtyRunState {
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(runStateRootRel)), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"dirty-root"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(runStateAttemptRel)), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"dirty-attempt"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
	}

	cancelCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
		Store: store,
		Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, id string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
			require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(id, bead.BeadEvent{
				Kind:      "interrupted-attempt-fixture",
				Summary:   "seed durable tracker mutation",
				Actor:     "worker",
				Source:    "test",
				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
			}))
			cancel()
			<-ctx.Done()
			return ExecuteBeadReport{
				BeadID:           id,
				AttemptID:        attemptID,
				Status:           ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
				Detail:           "context canceled",
				Error:            "context canceled",
				SessionID:        "sess-interrupted-attempt",
				BaseRev:          head,
				ResultRev:        head,
				ProjectRoot:      projectRoot,
				RequestedProfile: "smart",
			}, nil
		}),
	}

	cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
	result, err := worker.Run(cancelCtx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
		Once:        true,
		ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
		FinalizeDurableAudit: func(report ExecuteBeadReport) error {
			return FinalizeDurableAttemptAudit(projectRoot, store, report)
		},
	})

	return interruptedAttemptGitRun{
		projectRoot:        projectRoot,
		store:              store,
		candidate:          candidate,
		result:             result,
		err:                err,
		attemptID:          attemptID,
		runStateRootRel:    runStateRootRel,
		runStateAttemptRel: runStateAttemptRel,
	}
}

package agent

import (
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestNewServiceFromWorkDirPassesUnreachableEndpointsToService asserts that
// NewServiceFromWorkDir no longer filters configured endpoints by /models
// reachability. Both reachable and unreachable endpoints must be present in
// the service config so the agent service can surface them with appropriate
// status rather than DDx silently removing them.
func TestNewServiceFromWorkDirPassesUnreachableEndpointsToService(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())

	dead := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {
		http.Error(w, "bad gateway", http.StatusBadGateway)
	}))
	t.Cleanup(dead.Close)
	live := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{"data":[{"id":"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit"}]}`))
	}))
	t.Cleanup(live.Close)

	workDir := t.TempDir()
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName, "config.yaml"), []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
  repository:
    url: https://github.com/easel/ddx-library
    branch: main
agent:
  endpoints:
    - type: lmstudio
      base_url: %s/v1
    - type: omlx
      base_url: %s/v1
`, dead.URL, live.URL)), 0o644))

	sc, err := serviceConfigFromDDxEndpointsNoFilter(workDir)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, sc)

	// Both endpoints must be present: DDx does not filter by reachability.
	names := sc.ProviderNames()
	require.Len(t, names, 2)
	var hasLmstudio, hasOmlx bool
	for _, n := range names {
		if strings.Contains(n, "lmstudio") {
			hasLmstudio = true
			entry, ok := sc.Provider(n)
			require.True(t, ok)
			assert.True(t, entry.IncludeByDefault, "configured local endpoint must participate in automatic routing")
			assert.True(t, entry.IncludeByDefaultSet)
			assert.Equal(t, "fixed", string(entry.Billing))
		}
		if strings.Contains(n, "omlx") {
			hasOmlx = true
			entry, ok := sc.Provider(n)
			require.True(t, ok)
			assert.True(t, entry.IncludeByDefault, "configured local endpoint must participate in automatic routing")
			assert.True(t, entry.IncludeByDefaultSet)
			assert.Equal(t, "fixed", string(entry.Billing))
		}
	}
	assert.True(t, hasLmstudio, "unreachable lmstudio endpoint must be passed to service, not removed by DDx")
	assert.True(t, hasOmlx, "live omlx endpoint must be present")
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveDDXScratchDirs(t *testing.T) {
	now := time.Date(2026, 5, 8, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()
	scratchRoot := t.TempDir()

	livePath := filepath.Join(scratchRoot, "ddx-e2e-live")
	freshPath := filepath.Join(scratchRoot, "ddx-test-fresh")
	nonDDXPath := filepath.Join(scratchRoot, "plain-old-dir")
	writeExecutionCleanupCandidate(t, livePath, ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
		ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
		WorktreePath: livePath,
		Liveness: &ExecutionCleanupLiveness{
			ExpiresAt: now.Add(time.Hour),
		},
	}, map[string]string{"payload.txt": "live\n"})
	writeExecutionCleanupCandidateWithoutMetadata(t, freshPath, map[string]string{"payload.txt": "fresh\n"})
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(nonDDXPath, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(nonDDXPath, "keep.txt"), []byte("keep\n"), 0o644))
	old := now.Add(-2 * time.Hour)
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(livePath, old, old))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(nonDDXPath, old, old))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(freshPath, now.Add(-10*time.Minute), now.Add(-10*time.Minute)))

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.ScratchRoots = []string{scratchRoot}
	mgr.ScratchMinAge = time.Hour
	mgr.Now = func() time.Time { return now }

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.DirExists(t, livePath)
	assert.DirExists(t, freshPath)
	assert.DirExists(t, nonDDXPath)
	assert.Equal(t, 2, summary.ScannedScratchDirs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedScratchDirs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(2), summary.PreservedActiveScratchDirs)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "preserved_active_scratch_dir"))
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsExpiredTestOwnedWorktrees(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()
	staleProjectRoot := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "stale-project")
	activeProjectRoot := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "active-project")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(staleProjectRoot, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(activeProjectRoot, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))

	stalePath := filepath.Join(tempRoot, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-stale-foreign-20260508T120000-deadbeef")
	activePath := filepath.Join(tempRoot, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-active-foreign-20260508T120000-feedface")
	writeExecutionCleanupCandidate(t, stalePath, ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
		ProjectRoot:  staleProjectRoot,
		BeadID:       "ddx-stale-foreign",
		AttemptID:    "20260508T120000-deadbeef",
		WorktreePath: stalePath,
		Registered:   true,
	}, map[string]string{"scratch.txt": "stale foreign\n"})
	writeExecutionCleanupCandidate(t, activePath, ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
		ProjectRoot:  activeProjectRoot,
		BeadID:       "ddx-active-foreign",
		AttemptID:    "20260508T120000-feedface",
		WorktreePath: activePath,
		Registered:   true,
	}, map[string]string{"scratch.txt": "active foreign\n"})

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{
		worktrees: []string{activePath},
	})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())

	assert.NoFileExists(t, stalePath)

	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "removed_unregistered_temp_dir"))
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "preserved_foreign_registered_worktree"))
}

func TestDirtyDurableAuditPathsPreservesLeadingDotForTrackedFiles(t *testing.T) {
	status := " M .ddx/beads.jsonl\n" +
		"M  .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl\n" +
		"A  .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl\n" +
		"?? .ddx/attachments/ddx-example/events.jsonl\n"

	assert.Equal(t, []string{
		".ddx/beads.jsonl",
		".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl",
		".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl",
		".ddx/attachments/ddx-example/events.jsonl",
	}, dirtyDurableAuditPaths(status))
}

func TestCommitDurableAuditOutputsPreservesLeadingDotForUnstagedTrackedPaths(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := newDurableAuditProject(t)
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName)
	metricsDir := filepath.Join(ddxDir, "metrics")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(metricsDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "beads.jsonl"), []byte("initial\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(metricsDir, "attempts.jsonl"), []byte("initial\n"), 0o644))
	runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "add", ".")
	runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed durable audit files")

	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "beads.jsonl"), []byte("updated\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(metricsDir, "attempts.jsonl"), []byte("updated\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, CommitDurableAuditOutputs(projectRoot, "20260515T111500-pathspec"))

	status := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--", ".ddx/beads.jsonl", ".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl")
	assert.Empty(t, status)

	subject := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "log", "-1", "--pretty=%s")
	assert.Equal(t, "chore: update tracker (execute-bead 20260515T111500-pathspec)", subject)
	show := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "show", "--name-only", "--pretty=format:", "HEAD")
	assert.Contains(t, show, ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
	assert.Contains(t, show, ".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl")
}

func TestCommitDurableAuditOutputs_ConcurrentLockHolderRetriesAndRecovers(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_BIN", testutils.BuildDDxBinary(t))
	projectRoot := testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, "standard")
	attemptID := "20260606T081500-audit-lock-recover"
	dirtyPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "metrics", "attempts.jsonl")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dirtyPath), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(dirtyPath, []byte("dirty\n"), 0o644))
	headBefore := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")

	origTimeout := durableAuditGitTimeout
	origContextFactory := durableAuditCommandContext
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		durableAuditGitTimeout = origTimeout
		durableAuditCommandContext = origContextFactory
	})

	durableAuditGitTimeout = 100 * time.Millisecond
	var ctxMu sync.Mutex
	var firstContextAt time.Time
	durableAuditCommandContext = func() (context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {
		ctxMu.Lock()
		if firstContextAt.IsZero() {
			firstContextAt = time.Now()
		}
		ctxMu.Unlock()
		return context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), durableAuditGitTimeout)
	}

	locked := make(chan struct{})
	release := make(chan struct{})
	lockErrCh := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() {
		lockErrCh <- withTrackerLock(projectRoot, "durable_audit_test", func() error {
			close(locked)
			<-release
			return nil
		})
	}()

	<-locked
	time.Sleep(3 * durableAuditGitTimeout)

	commitErrCh := make(chan error, 1)
	go func() {
		commitErrCh <- CommitDurableAuditOutputs(projectRoot, attemptID)
	}()

	releaseAt := time.Now()
	close(release)
	require.NoError(t, <-lockErrCh)

	firstErr := <-commitErrCh
	if firstErr != nil {
		require.True(t, isTransientGitContention(firstErr), "expected transient contention error after lock contention, got %v", firstErr)
	}

	ctxMu.Lock()
	require.False(t, firstContextAt.IsZero(), "expected a git subprocess deadline context to be created")

import (

type interruptedAttemptGitRun struct {
	projectRoot        string
	store              *bead.Store
	candidate          *bead.Bead
	result             *ExecuteBeadLoopResult
	err                error
	attemptID          string
	runStateRootRel    string
	runStateAttemptRel string
}

func TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptCommitsTrackerCleanup(t *testing.T) {
	run := runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t, false)

	require.ErrorIs(t, run.err, context.Canceled)
	require.NotNil(t, run.result)
	require.Len(t, run.result.Results, 1)

	got, err := run.store.Get(context.Background(), run.candidate.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
	assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)

	status := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--",
		".ddx/beads.jsonl",
		".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl",
		".ddx/attachments",
	)
	assert.Empty(t, status, "interrupted cleanup must not leave tracker/audit paths dirty")

	subject := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "log", "-1", "--pretty=%s")
	assert.Equal(t, "chore: update tracker (execute-bead "+run.attemptID+")", subject)

	show := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "show", "--name-only", "--format=", "HEAD")
	assert.Contains(t, show, ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
}

func TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptDoesNotCommitRunState(t *testing.T) {
	run := runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t, true)

	require.ErrorIs(t, run.err, context.Canceled)
	require.NotNil(t, run.result)

	show := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "show", "--name-only", "--format=", "HEAD")
	assert.NotContains(t, show, run.runStateRootRel)
	assert.NotContains(t, show, run.runStateAttemptRel)

	status := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--", run.runStateRootRel, run.runStateAttemptRel)
	assert.Contains(t, status, run.runStateRootRel)
	assert.Contains(t, status, run.runStateAttemptRel)

	trackerStatus := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--",
		".ddx/beads.jsonl",
		".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl",
		".ddx/attachments",
	)
	assert.Empty(t, trackerStatus, "tracker cleanup commit must still land while run-state stays dirty")
}

func TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptDoesNotRecordTerminalOutcome(t *testing.T) {
	run := runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t, false)

	require.ErrorIs(t, run.err, context.Canceled)
	require.NotNil(t, run.result)
	require.Len(t, run.result.Results, 1)

	report := run.result.Results[0]
	assert.True(t, report.Disrupted)
	assert.Equal(t, "context_canceled", report.DisruptionReason)
	assert.Empty(t, report.RetryAfter)

	got, err := run.store.Get(context.Background(), run.candidate.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
	assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)
	assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
	_, hasRetry := got.Extra["work-retry-after"]
	assert.False(t, hasRetry, "cancelled attempts must remain immediately retryable")

	events, err := run.store.Events(run.candidate.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	for _, ev := range events {
		if ev.Kind != "execute-bead" {
			continue
		}
		assert.NotContains(t,
			[]string{ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied, ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges},
			ev.Summary,
			"interrupted cleanup must not record a terminal outcome event",
		)
	}
}

	candidate := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-interrupted-attempt", Title: "Interrupted attempt", Priority: 0}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), candidate))

	stalePath := filepath.Join(tempRoot, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-stale-foreign-20260508T120000-deadbeef")

		BeadID:       "ddx-stale-foreign",

assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedAgentLogs)

}

package agent

import (
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestNewServiceFromWorkDirPassesUnreachableEndpointsToService asserts that
// NewServiceFromWorkDir no longer filters configured endpoints by /models
// reachability. Both reachable and unreachable endpoints must be present in
// the service config so the agent service can surface them with appropriate
// status rather than DDx silently removing them.
func TestNewServiceFromWorkDirPassesUnreachableEndpointsToService(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())

	dead := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {
		http.Error(w, "bad gateway", http.StatusBadGateway)
	}))
	t.Cleanup(dead.Close)
	live := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{"data":[{"id":"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit"}]}`))
	}))
	t.Cleanup(live.Close)

	workDir := t.TempDir()
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName, "config.yaml"), []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
  repository:
    url: https://github.com/easel/ddx-library
    branch: main
agent:
  endpoints:
    - type: lmstudio
      base_url: %s/v1
    - type: omlx
      base_url: %s/v1
`, dead.URL, live.URL)), 0o644))

	sc, err := serviceConfigFromDDxEndpointsNoFilter(workDir)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, sc)

	// Both endpoints must be present: DDx does not filter by reachability.
	names := sc.ProviderNames()
	require.Len(t, names, 2)
	var hasLmstudio, hasOmlx bool
	for _, n := range names {
		if strings.Contains(n, "lmstudio") {
			hasLmstudio = true
			entry, ok := sc.Provider(n)
			require.True(t, ok)
			assert.True(t, entry.IncludeByDefault, "configured local endpoint must participate in automatic routing")
			assert.True(t, entry.IncludeByDefaultSet)
			assert.Equal(t, "fixed", string(entry.Billing))
		}
		if strings.Contains(n, "omlx") {
			hasOmlx = true
			entry, ok := sc.Provider(n)
			require.True(t, ok)
			assert.True(t, entry.IncludeByDefault, "configured local endpoint must participate in automatic routing")
			assert.True(t, entry.IncludeByDefaultSet)
			assert.Equal(t, "fixed", string(entry.Billing))
		}
	}
	assert.True(t, hasLmstudio, "unreachable lmstudio endpoint must be passed to service, not removed by DDx")
	assert.True(t, hasOmlx, "live omlx endpoint must be present")
}

^Ioriginator := bead.NewOperatorPromptBead("test prompt", bead.DefaultPriority)$
^Iif err := store.Create(context.Background(), originator); err != nil {$
^I^It.Fatal(err)$
^I}$
^Iaffected := &bead.Bead{$
^I^IID:        "op-aaaa1111",$
^I^ITitle:     "affected",$
^I^IStatus:    bead.StatusOpen,$
^I^IIssueType: "task",$
^I}$
^Iif err := store.Create(context.Background(), affected); err != nil {$
^I^It.Fatal(err)$
^I}$
$
^Inow := time.Now().UTC()$
^Iif err := recordOperatorPromptBacklinks(store, originator.ID, operatorPromptAffected{$
^I^IBeadIDs:   []string{affected.ID},$
^I^IArtifacts: []string{"docs/concerns.md"},$

^I^It.Fatalf("affected bead must carry exactly 1 origin_operator_prompt_id event, got %d", len(affEvents))$
^I}$
^Iif !strings.Contains(affEvents[0].Body, originator.ID) {$
^I^It.Errorf("affected event body must reference originator %q, got %q", originator.ID, affEvents[0].Body)$
^I}$
$
^IorigEvents, err := store.EventsByKind(originator.ID, OperatorPromptBacklinkEventKind)$
^Iif err != nil {$
^I^It.Fatalf("events on originator: %v", err)$
^I}$
^Iif len(origEvents) != 1 {$
^I^It.Fatalf("originator must carry exactly 1 blast-radius summary event, got %d", len(origEvents))$
^I}$
^Ibody := origEvents[0].Body$
^Iif !strings.Contains(body, affected.ID) {$
^I^It.Errorf("originator summary body must list affected bead %q, got %q", affected.ID, body)$
^I}$
^Iif !strings.Contains(body, "docs/concerns.md") {$
^I^It.Errorf("originator summary body must list artifact path, got %q", body)$
^I}$
}$
$
// AC: empty affected set is a no-op (no events appended). Used to verify$
// that an operator-prompt bead whose execution touched nothing does not$
// pollute the bead-event ledger.$
func TestRecordOperatorPromptBacklinks_EmptyAffectedNoEvents(t *testing.T) {$
^Idir := t.TempDir()$
^IddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName)$
^Iif err := os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755); err != nil {$
^I^It.Fatal(err)$
^I}$
^Iif err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte("version: \"1.0\"\nbead:\n  id_prefix: \"op\"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {$
^I^It.Fatal(err)$
^I}$
^Istore := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)$
^Iif err := store.Init(context.Background()); err != nil {$
^I^It.Fatal(err)$
^I}$
^Ioriginator := bead.NewOperatorPromptBead("noop prompt", bead.DefaultPriority)$
^Iif err := store.Create(context.Background(), originator); err != nil {$
^I^It.Fatal(err)$
^I}$
^Iif err := recordOperatorPromptBacklinks(store, originator.ID, operatorPromptAffected{}, "test", time.Now().UTC()); err != nil {$
^I^It.Fatalf("noop backlinks: %v", err)$
^I}$
^Ievents, err := store.EventsByKind(originator.ID, OperatorPromptBacklinkEventKind)$
^Iif err != nil {$
^I^It.Fatal(err)$
^I}$
^Iif len(events) != 0 {$
^I^It.Errorf("empty affected must append no events, got %d", len(events))$
^I}$
}$

data.text: I found the subtle bug: the new helper was returning the `.ddx` directory, but several callsites were treating it like the project root. That’s why some cleanup/resource tests started looking under `.ddx/.ddx/...`. I’m correcting the helper contract to return the root path and adjusting the couple of direct callers that need the `.ddx` subpath explicitly.
